12.1 OPENING OF DL/TL ACCOUNT 
(I) DL/TL Account Opening: 
This is the first step of DL/TL account opening process. Before opening of DL / TL/ CC/ OD account 
we should be ready with either CIF number or any existing account number from same CIF. Follow 
the below mentioned navigation for opening Demand Loan or Term Loan account. 
Navigation:  Advances->DL / TL Account and Services -> Create -> New Account -> SCR: 62000 
will appear ->Type CIF number/ existing Account number -> Select System as “LON: Create a 
Loan Account”.

type whenever required

the  minimum  required  details  viz.  Account  Type,  Interest  Category,  Sector /  Sub  Sector,

-> click Transmit button

onscreen 31682: Loan Product classification details.

as ‘Yes’ in SCR:31708: Loan Branch Exemption Details.

Note:  ZO 42 user type can change the Start date, Expiry date and Maximum Loan amount 
as per the loan terms. Verify the expiry date as per loan terms. Special care has to be 
taken for Housing loan, Education loan, Project Finance etc. where disbursement period 
is for a longer duration. 
At  the  time  of  Disbursement  Schedule  Creation - If  the  sum  of  amount  entered  in 
disbursement schedule (in screen No 12593) is less than the loan application amount, 
then  system will  throw  appropriate  error and will  not  allow  user  to  proceed  with  the 
transaction. 
At the time of Disbursement Schedule Amendment - If the sum of amount entered in 
disbursement  schedule  (in  screen  No  12593)  is  less  than  loan  approved  amount 
including additional approval if any, then system will throw appropriate error and will 
not allow user to proceed with the transaction.

12.2 GENERATION / AMENDMENT OF DL/TL REPAYMENT SCHEDULE 
Once Disbursement Schedule has been generated successfully then there is need for generation 
of repayment schedule as given below. 
Navigation: Advances->DL/TL Account and Services -> Loan Processing -> Generate Repayment 
Schedule -> New Screen (SCR:017034 Loans: Generate Repay Schedule Prompt) will appear as 
given  below.  Here  Type  Loan  Account  number  and  select  option  as  C:  Create.  Choose  the 
appropriate repayment Option as shown below and click on transmit. 
Note: In the Action field, by default C: Create will appear. Apart from this, 2 (two) other 
options, A: Amend and E: Enquire are available for amend or enquiry in the future. But 
for  new  account,  C:  Create  has  to  be  selected  (Amend  is  selected  when  repayment 
schedule already created is to be amended and Enquiry option is selected when we have 
to view repayment schedule which has already been created). In the Option field, there 
are  6  options  available  (viz.  EMI,  Principle  equally.  Distributed,  projected  interest, 
Negotiated Repayment, PMI, EHI/EYI). However, we normally select EMI, PrincEqDistr 
or EHI/EYI option 
Maximum types of schedules generated are EMI, PED, EMI/EHY and Negotiated as per 
repayment type and loan sanctioned. 
Note:  Any  DL  and  term  correction  of  loan  can  be  done  by  User  Type  42  only.  The  DL 
Correction  for  Accounts  limit  up  to  5Cr  is  to  be  done  by  FGMO  User  type  42,  and  for 
accounts of limit above 5Cr will be done from CO. 
Repayment Schedule Types: There are 6 different types of Repayment schedule option available 
in Bancs. The User has to select the appropriate repayment schedule type as per loan sanctioned 
terms and conditions. Explanation along with screenshot for all the repayment schedule type is 
given below: 
1. EMI 
2. Principal Equally distributed 
3. Projected Interest 
4. Negotiated Repayment 
5. EHI/EYI 
6. Progressive Monthly InstallmentChapter 12 LOANS & ADVANCES                                                                                                            Page|  577  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
(I) Equated Monthly Installment (EMI): 
(a) First Time Generation of EMI repayment Schedule:  
It is to be selected when the required frequency of installment is monthly with no moratorium or 
some moratorium period for complete installment or some moratorium for Principle Repayment 
with no moratorium for Interest Payment. 
Navigation: Advances->DL/TL Account and Services -> Loan Processing -> Generate Repayment 
Schedule -> New Screen (SCR: 017034 Loans: Generate Repay Schedule Prompt) will appear as 
given below. Here Type Loan Account number and select option as C: Create. Choose repayment 
Option as “1: EMI” and click on Transmit. 
Field Description Particulars / Value to be filled in / Selected by the User 
Account Number Loan Account Number

(b) “Negotiated  Repayment  Schedule”- Possible  Error  messages,  its  reason  and 
Solution: 
 
1. Error: 899: PRIN STRT DT-SEQUNCE/REPAY DAY INCORRECT 
Reason: This is due to mismatch in Repay Day (being shown in the Repay Day (disabled field) on 
Screen 17022) and Principle start day on the same screen.  
Solution: User should correct the Principle “start date” in such a way that it matches with Repay 
Day being shown, However if repay day is to be corrected then User should first correct the same 
by following  below given Navigation -> Once repay day correction done then only Negotiated 
repayment Schedule should be Created / Amended. 
2. Error: Please enter Valid Int Start date  
Reason: User is trying to enter the incorrect month/year in the `Int Start date’ field. e.g. if an 
account  is  opened  on  09/10/2021  and  the  Interest  Start Date  is  entered  as  102021(MMYYYY 
format) then the System will display the above message because the interest is going to be debited 
in the account only on 30/11/2021 before which interest repayment cannot be demanded. 
Solution: User should correct the Interest Repay Start Date 
3. Error: Select correct OLD/ Repay Option 
Reason: The existing Repay Type is different from the Option selected against `OLD Repay Option’ 
field. E.g. the existing Repay Type is `PrincEqDist’ for the loan account and while amending the 
schedule the user inadvertently selects the Option as `EMI to Negotiated’ Or `Negotiated to 
Negotiated’ Or Fresh Creation – Negotiated. In such case, the system will display the above error 
message. 
Solution: User should correct the Old / Repay Option type.Chapter 12 LOANS & ADVANCES                                                                                                            Page|  594  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
4. Error: Invalid Cycle 
Reason: If the value entered in the Cycle is 0 in either of the row. 
Solution: Correct the cycle value (i.e. Number of Repayment for that period)in all the rows. 
5. Error: Invalid Frequency or All Prin Repay Freq must be equal 
Reason: The value entered under Frequency field may be incorrect. For example, for the Principle 
repayment which will be made on quarterly basis and will be paid in 4 quarters the correct method 
of entering  is 4 under Cycle  and  3 under Frequency fields. If  it is entered  vice versa then  the 
system will display the above error message.  
Solution: The value in the frequency field should be 1 / 3 / 6 / 12. 
6. Error: Amounts Total should Tally with the Balance.  
Reason: The total amount of installments is NOT tallying with the amount appearing against the 
field `Total Principle Balance’ Or `Theo Loan Balance’ in the screen (SCR: 17022) as per the Option 
selected against `Arrive installmentamt on’ field.  
Solution: Please recheck and correct the installment amount entered on this screen. 
7. Error: Date beyond Loan End Date.  
Reason: Last repayment date is beyond the actual loan end date of the repayment schedule which 
is being generated. E.g. if a loan is sanctioned on 09/10/2021 for a period of 120 months then for 
this account the loan end date would be 01/12/2030. But, as per the repayment schedule which 
is being generated, if the last repayment date is 01/01/2031 or later then the system will display 
the above error message.  
Solution: Please recheck and correct the Principle Start date in last row. Accordingly, other rows 
should be corrected (if there is any mistake). 
8. Error: Invalid Action Request.  
Reason: When repayment type is selected either as EMI or PrincEqDist Option, for an account 
which is already existing under Negotiated Repayment, the system will display the above error 
message.

12.3 SECURITY CREATION / AMENDMENT/AUTHORIZATION 
Collateral  creation  should  be  done,  wherever  DL/TL/CC/OD/Bills  account  is  partially  or  fully 
secured.  
 In banking parlance, the term ‘Collateral’ in CBS refers to both ‘Primary’ and ‘Secondary 
(Collateral)’ Security.  
 Whenever the user wants the system to calculate Drawing Power, he has to select the type 
of security as “Primary”.  
 First screen is common for all types of security however second screen will vary based on 
collateral type. Value of security/DP is dependent on amount and margin entered in second 
screen. 
(I) Creation of Security: 
Creation of security involves two steps: 
Step1: Security Creation in 1st Screen (Main Screen) 
Step2: Security Detail updating in 2nd and 3rd Screen (Details Screen). 
 
Important points for entering proper values in collateral creation screen 
1) The Security Creation in 1st Screen (Main Screen) is common for any nature of security-
whether Bank Deposits, Paper based security, Stocks, land & building etc. 
2) Field “Status” should be kept “Active” (When a security is to be cancelled, it is made 
“Inactive) 
3) Appropriate “Collateral Type” is  to  be  selected  first  from  the  dropdown  and  then  related 
“Collateral Sub-type “is to be selected. 
4) Insurance required indicator is to be selected “Yes” or “No” depending upon the insurance 
availability (I.e. if Bank has to record insurance details then it will be “Yes”). 
5) Collateral short description should be entered. 
6) By default, Commencement date will be date of creation of security. Based on collateral type  
7) Expiry date will be auto populated / user input. There is no need to enter any value under 
“Review Date”.

against Primary Security, set the Link Flag to ‘Yes’, to enable the system to arrive at DP.

Points to remember - Multi Limit Overdraft against Deposit: 
1. In  Multi  limit  OD  against  Deposit, Any  number  of  term deposits  can be  used  for 
creation  of  limit,  however  maximum  interest  variance  of  Term  Deposits  is 
restricted to only 4. It means that similar interest rate Term Deposits should be 
clubbed together to form one limit with same ROI. 
2. Limit Approval Screen of “Multi limit OD against deposit Account” is different as 
compared  to  Normal  CCOD Account  Approval  Screen. Here  Screen (SCR:  007050 
Deposits:  Change  Details)  should  be  used.  Please  see  the  Approval  of  Limit  for 
“Multi Limit OD Angst Deposit” segment given above.  
3. Always arrange the OD limit details as per the ascending order of OD rate. It Means 
Lowest Interest Rate Term deposit group should be in first row, while the group 
with higher interest rate should be in next row and so on. 
4. 5 TDs can be included in single collateral. If there are more than 5 Term Deposits, 
then collateral is to be created. It should be done until all the Term deposits are 
entered in the collateral attached with Multi Limit OD Angst Deposit Account 
5. Rate increment details: 
If Term deposit rate is 4.4%, 5.1% and 6.4%,

12.9 KCC (Kisan Credit Card) MODULE 
Kisan Credit Card (KCC) Scheme is a Credit scheme prepared by NABARD on the Recommendation 
of R V Gupta Committee to provide financial support to the farmers of India. It was first introduced 
in August 1998 by Indian banks. Its Objective is to meet the comprehensive Credit Requirement 
of Agriculture, Fisheries and Animal husbandry. 
KCC Offers Credit facility to the farmers in two types as given below. 1. Cash Credit (to meet short 
term  credit  requirement  for  Crops)  2.  Term  Loan  (for  allied  activities  viz.  Pump  sets,  Land 
Development, Plantation and Drip Irrigations etc.) 
In This Regard our Bank  has developed  new KCC Module &  incorporated  the same in CBS  for 
processing and maintenance of KCC Accounts. For this purpose, Product Code 1995-0155 has been 
created for opening of new KCC Accounts & the earlier product codes has been discontinued i.e. 
15.02.2021. 
The following are the sub-menus under the KCC Module – 
KCC Document Revival 
KCC Registration 
KCC Account Creation 
KCC Limit Creation 
KCC Security Creation 
KCC Security Authorize 
KCC Limit Approval 
KCC Limit Amend 
KCC Security Amend 
KCC Enquiry/ Security SummaryChapter 12 LOANS & ADVANCES                                                                                                            Page|  718  
    NBC User Manual V1.0

CISLA Details to be updated/ created before Limit Creation. 
For Renewal of KCC A/c’s, following stipulations have been incorporated in the system – 
a. There should be NIL outstanding in the account or the account should have credit balance at 
the time of renewal. 
b. If the  account is in debit balance at the time of renewal, system would  check the following 
condition: - 
Debit Turnover (including interest & other charges) during the previous 12 months should be less 
than or equal to the total of Credit Turnover during the previous 12 months & up to the renewal 
date of the existing month. 
Therefore, renewal would be permitted only if the account satisfies either of the above criteria.

the Commercial Banks. Based on the RBS inspection conducted for FY 2019-20 in Indian Bank,

and availability of data, by carrying out data cleansing and ensure correct data in the CBS fields
